# qdedit

> A standalone, cross-platform desktop markdown editor with rich rendering and LLM chat integration.

## What is qdedit?

qdedit is a native desktop application built with Tauri v2 (Rust backend + webview frontend) and powered by quikdown for markdown rendering. It provides:

- Rich markdown editing with live preview
- Built-in LLM chat assistant ("QD") with tool calling
- Support for Mermaid diagrams, LaTeX math, GeoJSON maps, 3D STL, CSV tables, Vega charts, ABC music notation, SVG
- Project mode with file tree, persistent memory, and key-value store
- Auto-detection of local LLM servers (Ollama, LM Studio)
- Support for OpenAI-compatible APIs and Anthropic Claude

## Architecture

- Backend: Rust (Tauri v2) — handles file I/O, CLI parsing, LLM API proxying
- Frontend: TypeScript (Vite) — runs in Tauri's webview
- Rendering: quikdown standalone bundle (~8 MB) with all fence renderers included
- LLM: Tool-calling loop with 20+ tools for document/memory/KV/file operations

## Key Files

- src-tauri/src/commands/llm.rs — LLM API proxy with streaming
- src-tauri/src/commands/project.rs — Project state, memory, KV, file tools
- src-tauri/src/commands/fs.rs — File I/O commands
- src/chat/chat-ui.ts — Chat UI, tool dispatch, slash commands
- src/chat/providers.ts — LLM provider adapters (OpenAI, Anthropic)
- src/editor/editor.ts — quikdown editor wrapper
- src/settings/settings.ts — LLM configuration UI
- src/main.ts — App initialization, file operations

## LLM Integration Details

The LLM operates on raw markdown only — it never sees or produces HTML. API calls are proxied through the Rust backend to avoid CORS and protect API keys. The tool-calling loop has a 60-second timeout per call and supports abort via stop button.

### Available Tools

Document: document_read, document_write, document_replace, document_insert, document_undo, document_redo, document_get_selection
Memory: memory_read, memory_write, memory_append, memory_clear
Key-Value: kv_get, kv_set, kv_delete, kv_list
File (project mode): file_read, file_write, file_list, file_stat
